author: y4rr
content:
Result: added three new distributions to ferrotorch-distributions:

**Independent** (independent.rs, ~190 lines + 4 tests)
- Wraps any base Distribution<T> and reinterprets the rightmost N batch dims as event dims
- sample/rsample pass through (sample shape unchanged)
- log_prob and entropy sum over the reinterpreted dims via successive sum_dim calls (each iteration removes one trailing dim, no permute needed)
- The standard wrapper for turning Normal(loc=[B,K], scale=[B,K]) into a multivariate-style distribution whose log_prob is shape [B] — building block for VAEs and diagonal-Gaussian latents

**MixtureSameFamily** (mixture_same_family.rs, ~270 lines + 5 tests)
- Wraps a Categorical mixing distribution + a same-family components distribution
- log_prob = logsumexp_k( log mixing_probs[k] + components.log_prob[k](x) ), computed by tiling the value tensor along a new K-axis to broadcast against the components batch shape
- sample uses two-step procedure: draw component index from mixing, then gather the corresponding component sample
- rsample errors out (mixture sampling is not reparameterizable without Gumbel-softmax)
- entropy errors out (no closed form for general mixtures, matches PyTorch)

**LowRankMultivariateNormal** (low_rank_multivariate_normal.rs, ~245 lines + 6 tests)
- Σ = W W^T + diag(D) parameterization, the standard low-rank-plus-diagonal covariance
- Validates that loc=[d], cov_factor=[d, r], cov_diag=[d] all positive
- Currently builds the dense [d, d] covariance and delegates to MultivariateNormal::from_covariance for sample/rsample/log_prob/entropy. This is correct but O(d²) memory and O(d³) per-call — for moderate d (a few hundred). Documented in the source as a follow-up: the O(d r²) Woodbury fast paths can land later once needed; correctness lands first.

Tests: 15 new (4 Independent + 5 MixtureSameFamily + 6 LowRankMultivariateNormal). Coverage includes validation errors, log_prob correctness against analytical reference values (e.g. mixture of two symmetric Normals at midpoint), entropy summing for Independent, sample shape for all three, error paths for MixtureSameFamily's rsample/entropy, weighted mixture log_prob (asymmetric weights at component-favored point).

All 284 ferrotorch-distributions lib tests pass (was 269, +15). No new warnings.

Updated the distributions table in lib.rs docs to include the new types.
